text: Title of the paper The investigation of activity of 1-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-3-(4-methylphenyl)prop-2-en-1-one 3 using pressurized microwave energy to produce the heterocyclic derivatives with active methylene compounds In this study, the activity of chalcone derivative 1-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-3-(4-methylphenyl)prop-2-en-1-one 3 was investigated with the use of pressurized microwave energy as a green energy source toward active methylene compounds such as ethyl cyanoacetate, acetylacetone and thioglycolic acid. In situ reactions of chalcone 3 with the active methylene compounds were carried out at 70 °C under pressure with microwave irradiation to yield the corresponding derivatives namely 2-hydroxyphenylcyanopyridone, 2-hydroxyphenyl acetylcyclohexanone and thieno[2,3- c ]chromen-4-one derivatives. Also, reaction of chalcone 3 with hydrogen peroxide in stirring medium afforded chromen-4-one derivative. The structure of the obtained compounds were confirmed via FT-IR, 1 H NMR, 13 C NMR and mass spectra. In addition, the synthesized compounds showed excellent antioxidant activity similar to that of vitamin C, where the presence of the hydroxyl group in the compounds enhances the scavenger radical inhibition activity. The activity of compound 12 was also verified through molecular docking stimulation with two proteins (PDBID: 1DH2 and PDBID: 3RP8) where the compound showed a higher binding energy and shorter bond length compared to that of ascorbic acid. Moreover, DFT/B3LYP/6-31G (d,p) calculations were carried out to predict the physical properties of the compounds, and the compound 12 was confirmed through X-Ray single structure using Hirsh field analysis of the compound to identify the hydrogen electrostatic bond interaction, and correlation with the optimized structure of the compound through matching the bond length, bond angle, FT-IR and 1 H NMR spectra.
